title: Mass Quiz — Activity 6.8 (NCERT Grade 6 Science Ch. 6)
instructions_for_student: Answer each question. Read the reason on every correct pick. Wrong answers return with a hint tied to what you would see on a balance scale!

additionalProps.finalMessage: Mass = the amount of matter in an object. More matter packed in the same space means more mass. A kilogram is a kilogram — no matter what material it is!

questions.text: Three identical cups are each half-filled.
questions.sub:
Cup A → water · Cup B → sand · Cup C → pebbles
Which cup is heaviest?
questions.reason: Pebbles are denser than sand or water, so the same volume of pebbles has the most mass.
questions.hint: Look at the balance — pebbles tip the scale further than sand or water!

questions.text: Half a cup of cotton wool vs. half a cup of water.
questions.reason: Water is much denser than cotton wool. The same volume of water has far more mass.
questions.hint: The balance tips down on the water side — water molecules are packed tightly together!

questions.text: 1 kilogram of iron vs. 1 kilogram of feathers.
questions.reason: 1 kg is 1 kg! Mass is the amount of matter. The material and size do not change the stated mass.
questions.hint: The balance is perfectly level — both sides show exactly 1 000 g!

agent_readme.description: A three-question interactive quiz targeting the mass-vs-volume misconception from NCERT Grade 6 Science Chapter 6 (Activity 6.8). Each question displays an inline SVG scene (cups / balance scale / level balance) with labelled choices. Correct answers lock green with a one-line science reason; wrong answers shake, show a hint, then allow retry. A final summary reveals score, stars, and the key takeaway.
agent_readme.curriculum_link: NCERT Grade 6 Science — Chapter 6.3.5: How heavy or light? / Activity 6.8
key_concepts_tested: Density determines mass for equal volumes (Q1 — cups)
Same volume of denser material has more mass (Q2 — cotton vs water)
Stated mass is identical regardless of material (Q3 — iron vs feathers)
interaction_flow: Student reads question + views SVG illustration
Correct: button locks green, reason banner appears, Next button shown
Wrong: button shakes red for 2 s, hint appears, auto-resets for retry
After all 3 correct: Summary card with stars, score, key insight, Try Again

finalMessage.description: Text shown on completion card summarising the key learning. Defaults to mass = amount of matter.

questions.description: Override the built-in questions. Currently the scenes are hard-coded SVGs; this field is for metadata reference and future extensibility.
questions.note: Scene illustrations are generated from built-in SVG components (SceneCups, SceneBalance, SceneIronFeathers). To add custom questions with custom scenes, extend the TSX.

default_3_questions.description: Use built-in questions by omitting additionalProps entirely

additionalProps.finalMessage: Remember: mass measures how much matter is in an object — not how big it looks!
